title: Rework interactive issue browser (housekeeping:list)
description: The list command needs reworking to provide a continuous flow rather than exiting on empty results. It should guide the user through repo selection, label filtering (looping back on no results), issue selection with detail view, comments viewing, and next actions (back to list, start work, export JSON, or exit).
acceptance_criteria:
- On no matching issues, returns to label selection instead of exiting
- Shows issue detail view after selection (title, full description, metadata)
- Offers view comments option
- Action menu after viewing: back to list, start work, export, exit
close_reason: Reworked navigation flow: 'back' returns to filtered issue list, actions loop back after export/start/brief, added 'main menu' escape hatch at both issue list and detail levels.

title: AI integration: housekeeping:brief command
description: Walk the user through selecting a repo and issue, display the issue detail, then generate a Markdown brief for AI agent consumption. Includes: repo name, issue number, title, branch name, full body, human comments only (filtering bots), labels, assignees, and optional free-text context field. Output saved to user-chosen path with non-interactive --output option.
acceptance_criteria:
- Generates valid Markdown brief with all required fields
- Filters out bot/automated comments
- Includes skippable additional context prompt
- Supports --output for non-interactive use
- Defaults to project root for output path
close_reason: Already implemented: BriefCommand with bot filtering, --output option, additional context prompt. Tests passing.

title: Non-interactive export (--output option)
description: Add an --output option to housekeeping:export so it can run without prompting for a file path. Enables AI agents and scripts to export issue data in a single command. Falls back to interactive prompt when --output is omitted.
close_reason: Already implemented: ExportCommand has --output option, falls back to interactive prompt. Tests passing.

title: Bulk export of all open issues
description: A command to export all open issues for a chosen repository into a single JSON file. An AI agent could run one command, receive the full issue set, and begin triaging. Could be a flag on the existing export command or a dedicated housekeeping:export-all command.
close_reason: Already implemented: ExportAllCommand exports all open issues with comments, --output option. Tests passing.

title: Staleness detection for open issues
description: Compare open issues against recent commits and test results to determine whether an issue is still relevant. Parse git log for references to issue numbers, check whether related tests pass, and flag issues that may have been resolved without being closed.

title: Label statistics
description: Display stats for labels: number of open issues per label, and task completion counts where issues contain checkbox lists.
close_reason: Already implemented: LabelStatsCommand shows open issues per label and checkbox task completion counts.

title: Editable branch name on housekeeping:start
description: When running housekeeping:start, allow the user to edit or replace the suggested branch name before creation.
close_reason: Already implemented: StartCommand uses text() prompt with suggested branch as editable default.

title: Agnostic shell version (hk CLI utility)
description: Transition from the Laravel-dependent package to a portable, shell-based utility. POSIX-compliant (Bash/Zsh), using curl, jq, git, and fzf/gum for interactivity. Single-file distribution. Commands: hk list, hk show, hk start, hk export. Config via .hksrc, ~/.config/housekeeping/config, or env vars.

title: Label management (add/remove/create/delete)
description: Allow adding and removing labels on issues from the terminal. Also allow creating and deleting labels on a repository.
close_reason: Already implemented: LabelCommand has add/remove labels on issues and create/delete labels on repos.

title: Roadmap view (Blade/JSON endpoint)
description: A publishable Blade view or JSON endpoint that displays filtered GitHub issues as a visual roadmap. Could be used in project documentation or internal dashboards, grouped by label or milestone. May be out of scope if it shifts focus away from terminal-based workflow.
